titel: The Municipal Board, Bollebygds Municipality, June 16, 2026.

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Location and Time: Tingshuset, Bollebygd, 1:00 PM - 7:00 PM Members: Ulf Rapp (S) (Chair) Daniel Persson (SD) (Vice Chair) Stefan Hederdal (M) (Second Vice Chair) - Sections 116-124, 126-128, 130-137 Beatrice Ekelund (S) - Sections 116-124, 126-137 Helena Thelin (S) Tomas Bedö (M) Fredrik Olofsson (C) Michael Plogell (FR) Jonas Ahlgren (S) - Section 125, replaces Beatrice Ekelund (S) due to a conflict of interest. MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Minutes Prepared By: [Signature] Certificate of Authenticity: §116 Information Security and Data Protection (including a presentation of the new Data Protection Officer for the Borås region) - Information Subject: Today's Meeting Elin Forsgren, the new Data Protection Officer for the Borås region, is attending today's meeting and will present the Borås region's work on the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), as well as her role as Data Protection Officer.

Ulf Aspelin, the Information Security Coordinator, will provide an overview of the municipality's work on information security and data protection.

Attendees will have the opportunity to ask questions. The chairperson will thank those providing the information.

Page 5 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Signed by: (Justerandes - person verifying the minutes) Official Extract Confirmation: §117 KS2025/294 Guidelines for Wartime Organization in the Municipality of Bollebygd Decision: The Municipal Board approves the guidelines for the wartime organization, according to the proposal dated 2026-02-18. Background: The work of developing a wartime organization involves, in advance, determining which activities the organization needs to prioritize and be able to operate during heightened alert and, ultimately, wartime. It is not possible to maintain all activities; the focus should be on essential services and the tasks that arise during heightened alert. During heightened alert, municipalities and regions must take specific measures regarding the planning and direction of activities, working hours, and leave for employees. Municipalities and regions must transition to a wartime organization at the highest level of alert and have the plans necessary to maintain their operations. Working Committee's Proposal to the Municipal Board: The Municipal Board approves the guidelines for the wartime organization, according to the proposal dated 2026-02-18. Decision-Making Process: The chairperson asked if there were any proposals for a decision and determined that there were. Supporting Documents:

  • §106 KSAU Guidelines for Wartime Organization in the Municipality of Bollebygd

Page 6 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Extract Confirmation: §118 KS2026/119 Occupational Health and Safety Policy for the Municipality of Bollebygd Decision: The Municipal Board's proposal to the Municipal Council: The Municipal Council adopts the occupational health and safety policy for the Municipality of Bollebygd, dated 2026-04-29. The previous guidelines for occupational health and safety, which were adopted by the Municipal Council on 2009-04-29, § 65, are repealed with the adoption of the new occupational health and safety policy.

Background: The current guidelines for occupational health and safety for the Municipality of Bollebygd were adopted in 2009 and were designed in the format that policy documents for occupational health and safety typically had at the time. The guideline for occupational health and safety is considered equivalent to an occupational health and safety policy. The guideline has functioned well, but there is now a need to revise it and also change its name to occupational health and safety policy, both to adapt it to current requirements and because the Municipality of Bollebygd has received criticism from the Swedish Work Environment Authority (Arbetsmiljöverket). The criticism has been that the current guideline has been too vaguely worded and too general. Based on this, work began to develop a new occupational health and safety policy for the Municipality of Bollebygd. A working group consisting of representatives from various departments and the HR department developed a proposal for the new occupational health and safety policy.

It is of utmost importance that an occupational health and safety policy is supported throughout the entire organization and that employees have the opportunity to participate in its development. Therefore, the proposal for the new occupational health and safety policy was sent out for review to all workplace meetings and staff meetings. Page 8 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Certificate of Authenticity This policy applies to all workplaces in the municipality of Bollebygd. During staff meetings, the occupational health and safety policy was reviewed, and employees were asked to provide comments and suggestions for improvements. Many valuable suggestions were received. All comments were incorporated into a new draft, which was then sent out for consultation to all management teams in the municipality. Many good suggestions were also received from them. Based on this feedback, the final version of the occupational health and safety policy for the municipality of Bollebygd has now been developed.

Through collaboration in the central joint working group (CSG), all trade unions have supported the employer's proposal for a new occupational health and safety policy for the municipality of Bollebygd. The new policy aims to clearly create conditions for a good working environment and contribute to employees and managers feeling joy, security, well-being, and engagement in their work. A health-promoting and safe working environment is a prerequisite for being an attractive employer. No employee or user should suffer from ill health or come to harm. The working environment encompasses everything that affects the work – physically, organizationally, and socially.

The Working Committee's proposal to the Municipal Board: The Municipal Council adopts the occupational health and safety policy for the municipality of Bollebygd, dated 2026-04-29. Previous guidelines for occupational health and safety, which were adopted by the Municipal Council on 2009-04-29, § 65, are repealed with the adoption of the new occupational health and safety policy.

Decision-Making Process: The chairperson asked if a decision could be made and determined that it could. Page 9 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Certificate of Authenticity Supporting Documents:

  • §107 KSAU Occupational Health and Safety Policy for the Municipality of Bollebygd
  • Occupational Health and Safety Policy for the Municipality of Bollebygd
  • Municipal Council decision 2009, and the guidelines for occupational health and safety

To be sent to: Municipal Council Page 10 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Extract Confirmation: §119 KS2026/140 Political Party Funding 2026 - The Christian Democrats Decision: The Municipal Board's proposal to the Municipal Council: The Municipal Council decides to disburse political party funding of 43,808 SEK to (The Christian Democrats) for the year 2026.

Background: In December 2020, the Municipal Council established regulations for municipal political party funding in the municipality of Bollebygd. These regulations comply with the requirements of the Municipal Act regarding political party funding, stipulating that funding is only provided to political parties that are legal entities and hold seats in the Municipal Council. Furthermore, the regulations require each party to submit an annual written report demonstrating that the funding has been used to strengthen the political party's role in local democracy. This report must cover the period from January 1st to December 31st and be submitted to the Municipal Board administration within the specified timeframe. The report must include both the political party funding disbursed during the year and any funds the party may have saved from previous years. An independent auditor, appointed by the party, must review the report to ensure that the party's use of the funding aligns with the purpose of municipal political party funding. The auditor's report must be attached to the party's report. In a decision made in September 2017, the Municipal Council decided that political party funding should be decided on a continuous basis as individual parties submit the required documentation. Page 11 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Confirmation of Extract

The support for political parties has now been finalized. The party support consists of a contribution per party and a contribution per seat: 44% of the base amount per party 15% of the base amount per seat In 2026, the base amount is estimated to be 59,200 SEK.

Proposal from the Municipal Board Administration: The Municipal Council decides to disburse party support of 43,808 SEK to the Christian Democrats (KD) for the year 2026.

Decision Process: The chairperson asks if there are any proposals for the decision and determines that there are none. Supporting Documents:

  • Party Support 2026 - Christian Democrats
  • Audit Report for Municipal Party Support, KD, 2026

To be sent to: Municipal Council Page 12 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Extract Confirmation: §120 KS2024/264 Architecture Strategy for the Municipality of Bollebygd - Decision regarding consultation. Decision: The Municipal Board decided to send the architecture strategy for the Municipality of Bollebygd for consultation to the municipal committees (sustainability committee, association committee, youth committee, and the municipal pensioners' committee (KPR)), expert committees, the political parties represented in the municipal council, and the County Administrative Board of Västra Götaland, and to make it publicly available. The deadline for submitting comments to the Municipal Board is 2026-09-30. Background: In December 2024, the Municipal Board tasked the municipal board administration with developing an architecture strategy for the Municipality of Bollebygd. On May 27, 2025, (§ 76), the Municipal Board decided that the Municipality of Bollebygd should make a commitment within the framework of the action program for environmental goals – "Challenges for a Sustainable Västra Götaland," item BK6 – Municipalities develop architecture strategies. The architecture strategy has been developed as a municipal-wide, guiding document. It is not legally binding but should serve as support for long-term and coordinated decisions in the municipality's work with planning, construction, and management of the built environment. Purpose of the architecture strategy: The purpose of the architecture strategy is to define the municipality's vision for sustainable and high-quality physical development. The strategy is intended for politicians and Page 13 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Confirmation of Extract

The architecture strategy aims to support early decision-making and a holistic perspective in urban development. It is also intended to serve as a dialogue tool for discussions about architecture, design, and quality in the built environment, and to clarify the municipality's intentions towards, for example, architects, developers, and designers.

How the architecture strategy was developed: The work on the architecture strategy was carried out in close collaboration between a political steering group and the municipality's staff. On January 28, 2025 (item 6), the Municipal Board decided to establish a political steering group consisting of the Municipal Board's working committee and one representative from each other party with representation in the municipal council. The political steering group was responsible for the content of the strategy and had the task of ensuring broad political support and progress in the work.

The process involved a broad, dialogue-based approach. Citizen dialogue was conducted through open seminars, surveys of residents and young people, and children's forums. In addition, workshops were held with politicians and staff. The results of the dialogue and relevant background information formed the basis for developing the strategy's goals, strategies, and principles. The referral process and public display involve some additional work for the administration in terms of handling and compiling received comments. The work is expected to be completed within existing resources.

The architecture strategy is a guiding document and a dialogue tool for discussions about architecture and design. To ensure that the strategy has an impact in this role, the referral process and public display are considered important for broad acceptance and Page 14 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Confirmation of Extract: This is a verified extract for review and approval by the political bodies.

Proposal from the Working Group to the Municipal Board: The Municipal Board will send the architectural strategy for the municipality of Bollebygd for consultation to the municipal committees (sustainability committee, association committee, youth committee, and the municipal pensioners' committee (KPR)), expert committees, the political parties represented in the municipal council, and the County Administrative Board of Västra Götaland County, and will also make it publicly available. The deadline for submitting comments to the Municipal Board is 2026-09-30.

Decision-Making Process: The chairperson asked if a motion for a decision could be adopted, and determined that it could. Supporting Documents for the Decision:

  • §108 KSAU: Architectural Strategy for the Municipality of Bollebygd - Decision regarding referral.
  • Architectural Strategy, referral proposal, pages 260528.
  • Architectural Strategy - referral questions.
  • Cultural Heritage Inventory of the Municipality of Bollebygd, 260413.
  • Dialogue on the Architectural Strategy, Report, 260515.

To be sent to: Planning and Development Unit Page 15 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Certified by: [Signature] Confirmation of Extract: KS2026/132

§121 Detailed Plan for Bollebygds Prästgård 1:5 - Decision on Assignment Decision: The Municipal Board's proposal to the Municipal Council The Municipal Council allocates 0.5 million SEK to the Municipal Board in 2026 for the implementation of this project. Funding will be achieved through a reduction in the budgeted surplus.

Municipal Board Decision: The Municipal Board assigns the Municipal Board Administration to develop a detailed plan for Bollebygds Prästgård 1:5.

Background: On 2023-12-06 (KS2023/263 §140), the Municipal Council assigned the Municipal Board to initiate work on the existing school grounds, with the aim of, among other things, building a multi-purpose hall. This assignment was revised on 2026-05-07 (KS2026/6 §55) to expand the scope from solely the school grounds to also include Bollebygds Prästgård 1:5. This is because the project to build the multi-purpose hall is closely linked to the renovation and expansion of Bollebygd School. Within the school grounds, a new building for grades 7-9 will be constructed, while other facilities are planned to be renovated or demolished to provide students with more suitable spaces. Therefore, several steps remain in this transformation before space becomes available to build a multi-purpose hall within the school grounds. Locating the multi-purpose hall directly adjacent to the school grounds, rather than within it, could therefore lead to a faster completion. Page 16 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Confirmation of Extract

Furthermore, while work is underway on the new multi-purpose hall, the Municipal Council has also tasked the Technology and Services Committee with reviewing the use of the property located at Bollebygds Prästgård 1:5. A risk assessment, environmental survey, and geotechnical investigation have previously been conducted on the property at Bollebygds Prästgård 1:5. The risk assessment, which describes the risks associated with proximity to a fuel station, concludes that no protective measures are necessary if construction takes place 25 meters from Töllsjövägen. However, the construction of the multi-purpose hall will require this distance to be reduced, which would necessitate protective measures in the form of fire-rated facades and windows.

The environmental survey and geotechnical investigation have been analyzed by the municipality's consultants to determine if they need to be supplemented and to obtain an estimated cost for remediation. The analysis of the environmental survey concludes that a limited amount of additional sampling is needed to better define the contamination that has previously been identified, in preparation for potential remediation. In addition, sampling for PFAS in the upper soil layers should be conducted at 3-4 locations, and a visual environmental inspection should be carried out, which could potentially lead to further surface sampling if contamination is suspected. The completion of the environmental survey and the remediation of the property to enable the construction of a multi-purpose hall on the site is estimated to cost approximately 430,000 SEK. This includes all costs such as registration, contract work, transportation, and landfill fees. The analysis of the geotechnical investigation concludes that the suitability for construction on the site is generally considered good. However, the investigation needs to be supplemented with a cone penetration test (CPT), screw sampling, and a standard penetration test (SPT), which will... Page 17 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Confirmation of Extract For a cost of 80,000 SEK.

In addition, the Municipal Board administration has been in contact with the County Administrative Board of Västra Götaland and the Swedish Transport Administration to discuss issues related to coastal protection and traffic. The authorities cannot provide definitive answers at this stage, but no clear obstacles related to coastal protection and traffic were raised during the meetings. The Municipal Board administration will continue to work on these issues within the framework of the detailed development plan process, but does not see them as reasons to oppose the plan commission.

A detailed development plan process does not mean that the location of the multi-purpose hall has been finalized. The purpose of a detailed development plan process is to investigate whether the site can be considered suitable for the proposed purpose. At this stage, the administration cannot definitively know whether the site is suitable for a multi-purpose hall or not; this is the very purpose of a detailed development plan process. The work will begin by investigating the most critical issues. According to the current assessment, the most critical issues are geotechnics, soil conditions, stormwater management, and flood management.

The cost of preparing the detailed development plan is an operating expense and is not currently included in the Municipal Board's budget. The administration estimates the cost of preparation to be approximately 0.9 million SEK, which is slightly higher than normal due to several complicating factors that need to be investigated. Of these 0.9 million SEK, 0.5 million SEK are expected to be spent in 2026, and an additional amount of the same amount is needed for implementation.

Based on the information that the Municipal Board administration currently possesses, the administration assesses that issues related to pollution, geotechnics, traffic, and Page 18 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: (Signature) Confirmation of Extract:

Coastal protection issues should ideally be addressed within the detailed planning process. However, these and other related matters will continue to be investigated as part of the detailed planning work. Based on this, the municipal board administration believes it is appropriate to initiate a detailed planning process for the property Bollebygds Prästgård 1:5.

Adjournment: The meeting was adjourned from 3:00 PM to 3:10 PM.

Working Committee's Proposal to the Municipal Board: The municipal board tasks the municipal board administration with preparing a detailed plan for Bollebygds Prästgård 1:5. The municipal board proposes that the municipal council allocates 0.5 million SEK to the municipal board in 2026 for the implementation. Funding will be achieved through a reduction in the budgeted result.

Members' Proposals for Decision: Michael Plogell (FR) proposes that a decision be made to conduct supplementary geotechnical investigations, as detailed in the attached report, estimated at 80,940 SEK, and an environmental assessment at an estimated cost of 61,000 SEK, before any decision is made regarding the detailed planning assignment.

Decision-Making Process: The chairperson notes that there are two proposals: one from the working committee and one from Michael Plogell (FR). The chairperson puts the proposals to a vote and determines that the municipal board will make a decision according to the working committee's proposal. Supporting Documents: Page 19 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: (Signature) Confirmation of Extract:

  • §110 KSAU Detailed Plan for Bollebygds Prästgård 1:5 - Decision on Assignment
  • Detailed Plan for Bollebygds Prästgård 1:5, Preliminary Study (PM) on Geotechnical and Environmental Assessment
  • Detailed Plan for Bollebygds Prästgård 1:5, Preliminary Study (PM) on Geotechnical Investigation
  • Detailed Plan for Bollebygds Prästgård 1:5, Preliminary Study (PM) on Overall Environmental Assessment
  • Detailed Plan for Bollebygds Prästgård 1:5, Risk Analysis

To be sent to: Planning and Development Department, Municipal Council Page 20 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: (Signature) Extract Confirmation: §122 KS2024/189 Detailed plan for Låddekärrsbu 1:11, etc., northeast of the Grönkullen interchange; decision regarding review 3. Decision: The Municipal Board will send the detailed plan for Bollebygd, Låddekärrsbu 1:1, etc., northeast of the Grönkullen interchange, for a new review, with a minor editorial change to the property designation in the plan description.

Background: The purpose of the plan is to enable the development of a new business area. The area is currently undeveloped, which means that new roads and technical infrastructure will need to be constructed. The existing telecommunications tower and overhead power lines within the area are proposed to be relocated within the plan area. The detailed plan also aims to adjust the entrance to the area and ensure the protection of animal and natural values along national road 40. Buildings with a total area of approximately 65,000 square meters could be built. The draft plan is in accordance with the current comprehensive plan.

The Municipal Board previously instructed the then-municipal planning committee to begin detailed planning for Bollebygd, Låddekärrsbu 1:1, etc., on 2021-12-16, § 210. Consultations were conducted, and the review period was held from 2024-09-16 to 2024-10-07 and from 2025-11-10 to 2025-12-12. A total of 14 comments were received during the review period from authorities and stakeholders.

The County Administrative Board assesses that the detailed plan, in its current form, cannot be accepted and may be subject to further review upon adoption. There remain uncertainties regarding stormwater management and Page 21 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: (Signature) Confirmation of Extract

The County Administrative Board assesses that the plan's feasibility depends on developing an acceptable and traffic-safe solution for access and egress in consultation with the Swedish Transport Administration. Otherwise, the County Administrative Board believes that previous concerns have largely been addressed.

SGI (the Swedish Geotechnical Institute) assesses that the proposed plan is geotechnically acceptable. The comments from previous reviews regarding the risk of landslides and geotechnical issues have largely been taken into account, but SGI points out that certain texts in the Geotechnical Report (PM Geoteknik) should be updated to align with the latest version of the geotechnical report and should not contain conflicting information.

The Swedish Transport Administration assesses that the proposal continues to risk negatively impacting state infrastructure. Particular criticism is directed at the stormwater management, where the Swedish Transport Administration believes that it must be ensured that daytime and stormwater runoff does not burden the Swedish Transport Administration's ditches or culverts. Furthermore, the Swedish Transport Administration points out risks related to the design of intersections, capacity, and traffic safety, especially in the event of a potential four-lane intersection. The Swedish Transport Administration requests continued dialogue and more detailed documentation before approval, and clarifies that a co-financing agreement for measures in state-owned facilities must be signed before approval.

SÄRF (Southern Älvsborg Fire and Rescue Service Association) believes that previous concerns have not been fully addressed. Clarity is particularly requested regarding responsibility, ownership, operation, and maintenance of the fire water supply, as well as who will finance and implement the facility. Clarifications are requested in the plan description.

Page 22 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Certificate of Authenticity

Several residents and nearby businesses have expressed concerns that the proposed plan may lead to a deterioration of the living environment due to the proximity of industrial activities, with a negative impact on well-being, safety, and property values. Criticism has been directed at the basis of the noise assessment, and there are fears of increased noise levels and other disturbances such as light, dust, and vibrations, particularly at night and during the construction phase. Furthermore, concerns have been raised regarding the risks associated with increased heavy traffic, traffic safety, and the strain on nearby intersections. Concerns have also been expressed regarding the impact on groundwater and private wells, as well as deficiencies in the documentation and solutions for water and sewage systems. Concerns have been raised about the risk of spreading soil contaminants, including PFAS, during construction work, emphasizing the need for collaboration and protective measures. In addition, the documentation regarding natural, landscape, and cultural values is considered inadequate, and supplementary surveys are requested. There is also a need for more comprehensive risk assessments regarding vibrations, subsidence, and other disturbances during the construction process. Finally, objections have been raised to the planned relocation of utility lines and other technical infrastructure, which may involve intrusions onto private properties, as well as a general criticism of the planning process, citing that more issues should have been investigated at the detailed planning level.

Comments received from the Swedish Transport Administration (Trafikverket) and the regional administrative board (länsstyrelsen) have been addressed through a dialogue with the Swedish Transport Administration regarding access roads, stormwater management, heavy rainfall issues, and further investigation and changes to the site plan. The stormwater and water/sewage system assessment has been updated. Comments from SÄRF (an organization, likely environmental) have been addressed through a meeting and updates to the stormwater and water/sewage system assessment. Page 23 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Confirmation of Extract

Other comments raised have primarily been addressed in the review report. The changes to the development plan are of such significance that a new review of the detailed plan is required.

The developer is responsible for expenses related to planning, building permits, legal fees, development contributions for public land, installation of pipelines including a pressure boosting station for water and sewage, relocation of pipelines, relocation of a telecommunications tower, relocation and construction of an existing shared road, connection fees, handling of soil and materials, including necessary permits, geotechnical measures including rock engineering measures, any measures for habitat protection, noise reduction measures, remediation measures, applications for exemptions, and notifications/permits for, among other things, relocation of an existing ditch and construction within the block area.

The municipality receives income in the form of connection fees according to the current water and sewage tariff, as well as a special tariff for upgrading pipelines to the development area. The municipality incurs expenses for its share of the shared infrastructure for the road. In addition, the municipality incurs no other expenses beyond the operation of municipal facilities.

Discussions are ongoing between the developer and the relevant utility companies. The relocation of the overhead power line, including remediation of the land, will be funded by the developer. Power supply will be arranged in consultation with the relevant network operators. Each utility company will build the necessary infrastructure and enter into agreements with future property owners regarding connection to their respective properties.

Page 24 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Confirmation of Extract

The municipal board administration assesses that the detailed development plan is suitable to be sent out for a new review process, in accordance with the Planning and Building Act, Chapter 5, Section 18. The detailed development plan is not expected to have significant environmental impacts.

The working group's proposal to the municipal board: The municipal board shall send the detailed development plan for Bollebygd, property Låddekärrsbu 1:1, etc., located northeast of the Grönkullen interchange, for a new review process.

Decision-making process: The chairperson asked if there were any proposals for a decision, and it was determined that there were. The chairperson also announced that a minor editorial change would be made to the decision, in the form of an addition: "A minor editorial change will be made to update the property designation in the plan description."

Supporting documents for the decision:

  • §111 KSAU: Detailed development plan for Låddekärrsbu 1:11, etc., northeast of the Grönkullen interchange, decision regarding review process 3.
  • Report: Traffic study for the Bollebygd Logistics Park, 2026-06-04.
  • Illustration plan: Låddekärrsbu 1-11, Review process 3, 2026-06-04.
  • Detailed development plan: Låddekärrsbu, Review statement 2, 20260604.
  • Detailed development plan: Låddekärrsbu 1-11, Plan description, Review process 3, 20260604.
  • Report: Regarding the new plan boundary and excavation work, Afry, 2026-04-22.
  • Report: Soil environment for the expanded plan area, Mitta AB, 260422.
  • Report: Geotechnical investigation, Mitta AB, 2025-12-15.
  • Report: Stormwater and water/wastewater investigation, 2026-05-14.
  • Detailed development plan: Låddekärrsbu 1-11, Plan map, Review process 3, 2026-06-04.

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Extract Confirmation: §123 KS2026/129 Resolution to terminate the planning assignment for Getabrohult 1:2, etc., Getabrohult. Decision: The Municipal Board resolves to terminate the planning assignment for the detailed development plan concerning Getabrohult 1:2, etc., in Getabrohult.

Background: On 2009-06-25, in resolution §139, the then-Building and Environment Committee was tasked with planning a portion of Getabrohult 1:17 to enable an expansion of an existing business area. The planning area includes not only Getabrohult 1:17 but also a portion of Getabrohult 1:2, to ensure the municipality has a planning framework for future business establishments. The area is largely owned by the municipality; Getabrohult 1:17 is owned by HP Flügger AB.

On 2017-04-10, the then-Urban Planning Committee decided to divide the detailed development plan for Getabrohult 1:17, etc., into two separate detailed development plans after the plan had been subject to public consultation (SBN2017/170 §70). The new detailed development plans were Getabrohult 1:17, etc., and Getabrohult 1:2. On 2018-08-20, the Urban Planning Committee approved the acquisition of the property Getabrohult 2:10 (SBN2018/475 §148). Since the municipality has purchased a property adjacent to the current planning assignment, the municipality has decided to terminate the existing planning assignment and initiate a new one that also covers both Getabrohult 2:10 and Getabrohult 1:2.

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Extract Confirmation: §124 KS2026/128 Detailed Development Plan for Getabrohult 1:2 and Getabrohult 2:10 - Decision on Assignment Decision: The Municipal Board assigns the municipal board administration (planning and development unit) to prepare a detailed development plan for Getabrohult 1:2 and Getabrohult 2:10. Background: On 2009-06-25, in agenda item §139, the former building and environment committee was assigned to plan a portion of Getabrohult 1:17 to enable an expansion of an existing business area. The planning area includes, in addition to a portion of Getabrohult 1:17, also a portion of Getabrohult 1:2, which is owned by the municipality of Bollebygd, with the purpose of ensuring the municipality's planning readiness for business establishments. The planning area is largely owned by the municipality, while Getabrohult 1:17 is owned by HP Flügger AB. Consultations regarding the draft plan were conducted during the period of July 20 to September 13, 2015. Following the consultations, a preliminary archaeological investigation revealed the presence of six sub-areas with archaeological remains within Getabrohult 1:2. The interested party who had previously expressed interest in developing Getabrohult 1:2 subsequently chose to withdraw. Therefore, further archaeological investigations are required for any further development within the property. Page 29 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Certificate of Authenticity

On 2017-04-10, the then-Urban Planning Committee (resolution § 70, SBN2017/170) decided to divide the detailed development plan for Getabrohult 1:17 and others into two separate detailed development plans, following the consultation phase. The new plans would cover Getabrohult 1:17 and others, respectively, and Getabrohult 1:2. Subsequently, on 2018-08-20 (resolution § 148, SBN2018/475), the Urban Planning Committee approved the acquisition of the property Getabrohult 2:10. Given that the municipality has now acquired additional land adjacent to the area covered by the existing development plan, it is considered appropriate to terminate the current planning assignment and instead initiate a new planning assignment that also includes Getabrohult 2:10 and Getabrohult 1:2. The investigations carried out as part of the previous planning work, particularly for Getabrohult 1:2, can be utilized in the continued planning process. This work aims to strengthen the municipality's planning preparedness and enable future developments when new stakeholders express interest in establishing businesses in the municipality of Bollebygd.

The planning area covers approximately 22 hectares; see the supporting documents for the decision. In the municipality's comprehensive plan, the area is designated as FÄ1, "An opportunity to create a cohesive business area. Expansion requires detailed planning." The planning area is considered strategically suitable for businesses, due to its favorable location near the Grönkulle interchange, which connects to national road 40. The area also offers an attractive signage location, which is highly desirable among companies looking to establish themselves.

The investigations carried out in the previous planning work can be used in the new planning assignment. The planning work will be funded by the municipality, and a planning fee will be charged at the time of building permits, after the sale of the land.

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Extract Confirmation: §125 KS2026/133 Plan Prioritization 2026 Decision: The Municipal Board decides to prioritize planning projects according to the proposed order listed under the "Assessment" section. The Municipal Board decides to replace the previous list and decision (KS2026-02-04, § 6) with the prioritization list in this decision.

Background: The Municipal Board Administration works on projects assigned to the administration by the Municipal Board. These assignments are then prioritized through a planning prioritization process. The most recent planning prioritization, an amendment to the decision from June 17, 2025, § 116, was approved by the Municipal Board on February 4, 2026, § 6. Since then, changes have occurred, and new planning assignments have been given to the administration by the Municipal Board. The Municipal Board should decide on a new planning prioritization annually. The Municipal Board Administration proposes that planning assignments are generally prioritized based on the following principles:

  • Plans that are of great importance for the municipality's development, concerning the municipality's operations and/or are of significant public interest.
  • Plans that contribute to the implementation of the comprehensive plan and municipal goals, as well as strategic plans that contribute to a deeper understanding of the comprehensive plan.
  • Plans that contribute to a large number of new housing units.
  • Special plans that are of significant public interest but do not involve housing or operations, such as infrastructure investments.

New planning assignments that have been added since the previous decision relate to municipal planning preparedness for businesses and municipal operations. Case: Buagärde Page 32 of 60

Background: The departments have prepared financial forecasts for 2026. The completed review has usefully highlighted the municipality's actions, supervisory responsibilities, and internal organization. Regarding compliance with agreements, this includes an inspection of the leased land (JB8:13 and section 8 of the agreement, and neighboring properties (section 8:3 and 13.1 of the agreement)). Subject: On December 2, 2025, the Municipal Board decided to conduct an independent external review of the operations conducted on properties Buagärde 1:2 and 1:9 (Råssa 1:11). The investigation revealed shortcomings in the municipality's actions as the property owner. Therefore, the Municipal Board needs to develop an action plan outlining how the municipality, as the property owner, should proceed in the future. The investigation also indicates shortcomings in the Building and Environmental Committee's (formerly the Urban Planning Committee's) supervisory and case management processes. The investigation states: "The review reveals a pattern of deficiencies in the internal processes for supervisory and case management. To ensure consistent, legally sound, and efficient handling in the future, the basic procedures need to be strengthened." The Building and Environmental Committee therefore needs to develop an action plan to ensure consistent, legally sound, and efficient handling in the future.

The investigation indicates both legal and financial risks associated with the municipality's actions. In connection with the interim report, the municipality will need to decide whether to set aside funds to cover potential future costs for restoration or compensation claims. Page 42 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Extract Confirmation:

To implement the proposed action requires a more in-depth review focusing on the economic risks. Based on the investigation, it is clear that the municipality needs to take action and review its procedures, both in its role as a property owner and as a supervisory authority. The Municipal Board and the Building and Environment Committee therefore need to develop action plans to ensure correct procedures going forward.

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Official Extract §130 KS2026/10 Response to Motion (FR) regarding mobile recycling stations Decision: The Municipal Board's proposal to the Municipal Council: The Municipal Board approves the proposal from the Technology and Services Committee; The Municipal Council rejects the motion. Decision Process: The chairperson asked if there were any proposals for a decision and determined that there were.

Subject: Michael Plogell (FR) proposed in a motion received on 2026-01-12 that the municipality of Bollebygd investigate whether there is a need to introduce a mobile recycling system. If it is deemed that there may be a need for a mobile system, it is proposed that it be procured. The municipality should then investigate whether the system can be funded, in whole or in part, by EU funds. The Municipal Council, at a meeting on 2026-02-19, § 6, referred the motion to the Technology and Services Committee for processing. The Technology and Services Committee considered the matter at a meeting on 2026-05-13, § 30, and proposes that the Municipal Council reject the motion. Implementing and operating a mobile solution would entail significant costs for the municipality, related to procurement, operation, handling of hazardous waste, and staffing. Neighboring municipalities with similar solutions, such as the municipality of Mark, will be reviewing and revising their mobile recycling centers during the year. Page 47 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Certificate of Authenticity

The utilization rate is not in balance with the high costs of the service. Bollebygd is a geographically small municipality with short driving distances to the existing recycling center located at Råssa. Through collaboration with neighboring municipalities, residents can also choose from several nearby facilities, which already offer a good level of service. The possibility to sort and recycle certain types of electronic waste (e.g., batteries and light sources) will increase when the municipality introduces a new collection system with 4-compartment containers and associated electrical boxes.

The Working Committee's proposal to the Municipal Board: The Municipal Board approves the Technology and Service Committee's proposal; The Municipal Council rejects the motion.

Decision-Making Process: The chairperson asks if proposals for a decision can be adopted and determines that they can. Supporting Documents:

  • §118 KSAU Response to motion (FR) regarding mobile recycling stations

MEETING MINUTES City Council Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Certified by: [Signature] Certificate of Authenticity: §133 KS2023/92 Evaluation of the new board and administrative structure following the division of the urban planning board. Decision: The City Council Board acknowledges the information and adds it to the records.

Background: On 2025-09-02, in item §148, the City Council Board assigned the City Council administration the task of evaluating the new board and administrative structure that came into effect on 2024-01-01.

Background: On 2023-11-16, in item §126, the Municipal Council decided to establish a new board and administrative structure for the former urban planning board/urban planning administration. This decision involved the creation of a new board – the building and environment board – with its corresponding administration, the building and environment administration. The remaining units of the former urban planning administration; water and sewage/streets, parks/waste management, catering, building maintenance, and operational properties, formed a new board – the technology and service board – with its corresponding administration, the technology and service administration. The property unit was divided into a strategic part, which was moved to the City Council Board (KS), and an operational part, operational properties, which then belonged to the technology and service board. The planning and development unit (PLEX) was moved to the City Council Board (KS).

Page 51 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: (Signature) Certificate of Authenticity

The cost of the new organizational structure was calculated before its implementation and served as the basis for the decision. It was likely that the new Building and Environment Committee would have more committee meetings than the previous conflict of interest committee. The number of members and substitutes in the new committee would be at least five plus five. This represented an increase in costs compared to the conflict of interest committee's budget, due to increased compensation of 600,000 SEK.

The former Urban Planning Committee, now the Technology and Services Committee, could be reduced to five members and five substitutes. The new Building and Environment Committee could have seven members and seven substitutes.

The Building and Environment Department would have a department head who would also be the first-line manager for caseworkers and other employees in the department. The cost increase, based on the previous budget, from an unit manager to a department head was estimated to be 250,000 SEK.

The Building and Environment Committee would need a committee secretary and registrar. These functions could be combined or included in an administrative/coordination position. This administrative position was an expansion and was estimated to cost 700,000 SEK.

In total, the estimated cost increase was 1,550,000 SEK.

The remaining units; Water/Sewer, Roads, Parks/Waste Management, Catering, Cleaning Services, and Property Management, operating within the current Technology and Services Department, should reasonably result in a change in the department's budget. It was proposed to eliminate the deputy department head position due to the reduced scope of the department. Budget: -1.3 Page 52 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Confirmation of Extract: Regarding the deputy administrative director, it is proposed that the increase in costs be offset against the above. The cost increase is calculated as: ((700+250+600)-1300) = 250,000 SEK. The relocation of PLEX to the Municipal Board was estimated to result in a smaller cost increase of 100,000 SEK, as a new organizational structure was needed within the Municipal Board administration (KSF). The division of the former property unit into operational and strategic divisions resulted in a smaller cost increase because a new management position was expected to be created within the operational unit, amounting to 200,000 SEK. The total cost increase for the entire reorganization was estimated to be: 250,000 SEK + 100,000 SEK + 200,000 SEK = 550,000 SEK. Evaluation: The evaluation materials were sent to the Building and Environment Committee, the Technology and Services Committee, the Municipal Board, Vision Bollebygd, Akademikeralliansen, and Kommunal. Evaluations were received from the Building and Environment Committee, the Technology and Services Committee, the Municipal Board, and Vision Bollebygd. In summary, it is perceived that the new organizational structure has predominantly positive effects, but there is a clear common theme regarding what is perceived as a less positive effect. It is noted that the flow of information, transparency, and dialogue in matters and between committees and administrations has decreased with the new structure. Previously, there was a cohesive committee and administration, both organizationally and physically, and elected officials in the former committee and staff in the former administration naturally had a broader overview. A recommendation from the evaluation is to strengthen dialogue, information sharing, and transparency between the Building and Environment Committee, Page 53 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Official Extract Issued by: The Technology and Services Committee, the Municipal Board, and their respective departments. Costs Prior to the reorganization, it was noted that several significant budget items would be affected, and that a net additional funding of 550,000 SEK would be required for the entire change. Below is a breakdown of the identified items and how the actual costs turned out. Increased remuneration for elected officials: 0.6 million SEK. The increased remuneration for elected officials increased by approximately 450,000 SEK between 2023 and 2025. After accounting for a 3% annual increase, the effect of the reorganization resulted in a cost increase of approximately 300,000 SEK, which is approximately 300,000 SEK lower than expected. Unit manager, construction and environment, becomes department head: 250,000 SEK. The increase in salary costs was approximately 100,000 SEK higher than expected. Administrative position, construction and environment department: 700,000 SEK. This item is difficult to track because several changes occurred during the same period, and it is not entirely clear what is directly attributable to the organizational change versus other factors. However, the initial estimate is likely relatively accurate. Assistant department head, technology and services, resigns: 1.3 million SEK. This was initially implemented and produced the expected effect. However, subsequent budget decisions included additional funding (see below) that partially replaced the position. PLEX to the Municipal Board: 100,000 SEK. This was implemented in accordance with the decision. Expansion of operational property management: 200,000 SEK. When the responsibility for strategic property management was transferred to the Municipal Board, the associated budget was also transferred. In order to maintain a full-time property manager, an additional 200,000 SEK was required. This has been implemented in accordance with the decision.

In total, the Municipal Board administration estimates that the cost increase is 0.2 million SEK lower than expected, primarily due to lower remuneration costs than initially calculated, but Page 54 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: [Signature] Extract Confirmation: It should be noted that other cost-driving initiatives/measures have emerged, particularly during 2025, which to some extent may be a consequence of the reorganization.

Other Effects Strategic Property An increase in costs has occurred in connection with the transfer of the strategic property portfolio to the Municipal Board. This increase consists of an additional 500 + 300 thousand SEK for investigations, and the hiring of an additional project manager. At the same time, the capacity to work with the municipality's new construction of facilities has been strengthened, and the accounting of operating and investment expenses is accurate. The administration believes that the cost increase is due to deliberate investments rather than the reorganization itself. Despite the transfer of the strategic part of the property function and all new construction projects to the Municipal Board, the resources for the property unit within the technology and service department have remained unchanged, thanks to the additional funding of 200 thousand SEK. The responsibility for local strategy has been transferred from one of the economists to the project manager, which means that the economic resources have increased slightly beyond what was planned for before the reorganization.

PLEX During the transition, certain tasks have been streamlined, resulting in the transfer of an administrator from PLEX to the HR department to strengthen the work in the reception area. This is a positive effect that has not resulted in any cost increases, but has allowed more tasks to be handled within the Municipal Board's area, including the implementation of extended opening hours in the reception, all within the existing budget. Budget Decisions After the Reorganization Page 55 of 60

MEETING MINUTES Municipal Board Meeting Date: 2026-06-16 Countersigned: (Signature) Certificate of Authenticity

The following budget decisions have been made after the reorganization, and the administration believes that at least some of these decisions may be partially a result of the reorganization. The Building and Environment Committee received an additional allocation of 400,000 SEK for an administrative coordinator in 2025. On the budget day in May 2026, the need for further strengthening of the administration was also presented. Funding has also been approved for more environmental officers, but the administration does not believe that these are a consequence of the reorganization. The Technology and Services Committee received an additional allocation of 800,000 SEK for a development manager in 2025. In total, a budget of 400,000 SEK + 800,000 SEK = 1.2 million SEK has been allocated to the Building and Environment Committee and the Technology and Services Committee for 2025, which may be a result of the reorganization, but it cannot be said with certainty that the increases are due to the reorganization.

The Labor Committee's proposal to the Municipal Board: The Municipal Board acknowledges the information and adds it to the records.

Decision-Making Process: The chairperson asked if there were any proposals for a decision, and it was determined that there were. Supporting Documents:

Page 60 of 60
tldr: The municipality of Bollebygd has decided on new regulations regarding how the municipality will operate in the event of war or heightened alert. They have also approved new plans for construction and architecture within the municipality.
lattlast:
The municipal council has approved new guidelines for how the municipality should organize its operations in the event of war or heightened alert. This means that the council has proactively determined which aspects of the municipality's work are most important to prioritize if the situation in society becomes unstable. The goal is to ensure that essential community functions can continue to operate even during difficult times.

Several decisions have been made regarding construction and urban planning. These include commissioning detailed plans for areas such as Bollebygds Prästgård and Låddekärrsbu. A detailed plan is a document that specifies what can be built and how the land can be used.

The municipality has also approved a new strategy for architecture. This is a plan for how buildings and environments should look and be designed in the future. Furthermore, the council has decided to terminate a planning project in the Getabrohult area and initiate new projects to plan the land there.

Finally, the council has received information about how the municipality works with data protection and security. The new data protection officer from the Borås region presented how the municipality complies with the regulations for personal data (GDPR) to protect residents' information.
